description:
Offered: may be less than once every two years
The course is a study of the development of modern art criticism. The course begins by examining traditional art criticism and then concentrates on contemporary art and criticism, including Clement Greenberg in the 1940s and concluding with Postmodern criticism of the 1980s and 1990s.
Prerequisite(s): One Art History course or MUSC 2000 .
prerequisites: One Art History course or MUSC 2000 .
